commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove unused network API dependencies

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: HaiClaude
repository.description: Native Haiku GUI launcher for Claude Code

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(phase-06): complete phase execution — all 9 must-haves verified, SUMM-01..07 delivered

commit.message:
Classify QueueConcatElaborates → §7.10.4 [skip ci]

The test assigns an unpacked array concatenation `{1, 2, 3}` to a queue variable `int q[$]`. §7.10.4 is titled "Updating a queue using assignment and unpacked array concatenation" and directly governs this pattern. The existing test comment `§10.10` is less specific — §10.10 covers unpacked array concatenation in general, but §7.10.4 is the most specific subclause for the queue-assignment case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add chunked transcription for long videos to avoid OOM

Whisper transcription of 4+ hour videos caused OOM (11GB RSS) when
processing the full audio in one pass. New transcribe_long_video()
splits audio into 30-min WAV chunks, transcribes each separately,
and merges results with corrected timestamps. Memory stays at ~1.5GB.

Tested on a 4h24m video: 9 chunks, 6129 segments, 15 sections identified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: ForgedCity Escape Room Engine

Full-stack cooperative escape room game with AI-generated puzzles.

Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + WebSocket rooms + Claude AI for puzzle
generation + Google Gemini for image generation.

Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS + Zustand.

Features:
- 12 puzzle pattern types with content schemas
- Real-time multiplayer via WebSocket
- AI-generated themed puzzles with inline content images
- Multiple choice (patterns 3,4,8,9) and free text answers
- Answer feedback toasts with auto-advance
- 1 hint per game team limit
- Content safety filter for generated puzzles
- Holy grail victory reveal

commit.message:
fix(optalocal): remove personal infrastructure from product code and docs

Removes hardcoded references to Mono512 (192.168.188.11), Opta48, and
personal paths across 8 apps. Key changes:

- CLI: ssh.user default '' (was 'opta'), ssh.pythonPath 'python3' (was
  personal venv), FallbackProvider renamed to MultiProvider with neutral
  language
- Code Desktop: ModelsStudio, App.tsx, ProjectPane all use generic
  defaults ("My Device", "your LMX server") instead of personal machine
  names
- LMX: _enforce_opta48_no_local_models() replaced with configurable
  _enforce_host_deny_list() via LMX_HOST_DENY_LIST env var
- LMX Dashboard: placeholders use localhost:1234 instead of personal IP
- Init: magic link defaults to localhost, workspace templates use generic
  examples
- Learn: all 7 guide files (5 HTML + 2 TS) updated to localhost:1234
- SettingsModal: daemon messaging updated for cloud-only user parity

32 files across 1D, 1L, 1M, 1O, 1P, 1V, and workspace docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

to align with the design system's tokenized color approach.

commit.message:
fix: remove sponsor preservation on re-analysis

Old sponsor links from hallucinated Gemini-only detections were being
re-added on re-analysis when sidecar found 0 sponsors. Since sidecar
OCR with bbox validation is now authoritative, 0 sponsors = 0 sponsors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix live waveform, harden security, remove hardcoded values

- Fix live waveform flat line: start auris-stream before auris-record
  (record.sh reads from Icecast), stop stream only when not listening
- Revert record.sh to Icecast -c copy (avoids ALSA device contention)
- LiveWaveform retries fetch while waiting for Icecast stream
- Talkback graceful drain: stdin.end() instead of SIGKILL on stop
- Security: replace all exec() with execFile() (no shell interpolation)
  in systemctl.ts, alsa.ts, device-config.ts; add input validation
- WebSocket talkback auth: verify JWT session cookie in server.ts
- Remove hardcoded credentials: Icecast passwords use placeholders,
  setup.sh generates random values and stores in /etc/default/auris
- Remove deployment-specific values: nginx server_name, sudoers username,
  ALSA device defaults, PM2 port all now configurable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Polymarket fee assumption; rewrite backtest to JS-level rigor

- Polymarket charges $0 fees on mention markets (only crypto/sports
  have taker fees). Remove incorrect 2% settlement fee assumption.
- Kalshi: $0.02 round-trip per contract (verified Mar 2026 schedule).
- Rewrite robustness_backtest.py: proper friction decomposition
  (gross → fees only → fees+1¢ slip → fees+2¢ slip), slippage
  sensitivity chart with breakeven analysis, 7 publication-quality
  plots, assumptions table with sources.
- Edge survives: μ=$0.102/contract net of fees+1¢ slip, bootstrap
  95% CI [$0.068, $0.123] excludes zero, 5/9 series sig at p<0.05.
- Fix pitch PDF fee section to match verified fee schedule.

commit.message:
add distance sensor integration to wake/sleep station_config in Supabase

Polls HC-SR04 on GPIO 23/24 every 0.5s. Wakes the station (station_asleep=false)
immediately on detection within 30cm. Sleeps it (station_asleep=true) after 30s
with no detection. Only writes to Supabase on state transitions. MockDistance
used on non-Pi hardware. GPIO cleaned up on shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cooldown countdown not showing and areas missing for switch entities

- Preserve cooldown_until when recording motion_cleared so the UI
  countdown survives the motion-cleared event that fires seconds after
  detection (both state-based and ZHA handlers).
- Include switch.* entities (with a device) in area discovery so areas
  containing only power outlets appear on the home page and organize mode.

commit.message:
Fix fail2ban start button and sudo -n for all commands v3.13.87

Start button was calling sudo systemctl start fail2ban which is not in
sudoers. Replace with fail2ban-client start which is already allowed.
Also add -n flag to all sudo calls in run_fail2ban_command so they fail
fast instead of hanging on a password prompt in the web process.
